context:
CREATE TABLE customers (
    cust_id   INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    name      TEXT NOT NULL,
    email     TEXT NOT NULL,
    region    TEXT NOT NULL,
    plan      TEXT NOT NULL,
    joined    DATE NOT NULL,
    rep       TEXT
);

CREATE TABLE products (
    sku       TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    product   TEXT NOT NULL,
    cat       TEXT NOT NULL,
    price     INTEGER NOT NULL
);

CREATE TABLE orders (
    oid        INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
    cust_id    INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES customers(cust_id),
    sku        TEXT    NOT NULL REFERENCES products(sku),
    dt         DATE    NOT NULL,
    qty        INTEGER NOT NULL,
    unit_price INTEGER NOT NULL,
    discount   REAL    NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    revenue    INTEGER NOT NULL,
    refunded   INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0
);

COLUMN GLOSSARY:

customers:
  cust_id : Unique customer identifier
  name    : Full name of the customer
  email   : Email address of the customer
  region  : Geographic sales region
  plan    : Subscription plan tier
  joined  : Date the customer signed up
  rep     : Sales representative assigned to the customer

products:
  sku     : Stock-keeping unit code
  product : Name of the product
  cat     : Product category
  price   : List price of the product

orders:
  oid        : Unique order identifier
  cust_id    : Customer who placed the order
  sku        : Product that was ordered
  dt         : Date the order was placed
  qty        : Number of units ordered
  unit_price : Price per unit at time of order
  discount   : Discount applied to the order
  revenue    : Revenue collected for the order
  refunded   : Whether the order was refunded
